message:
🌱 etcd: etcd go client fails when querying a cluster with a down node (#1943)

* 🌱 Add etcd: etcd go client fails when querying a cluster with a down mission

* 🌱 Add etcd: etcd go client fails when querying a cluster with a down mission

Signed-off-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

@@ -0,0 +1,76 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "etcd-9949-etcd-go-client-fails-when-querying-a-cluster-with-a-down-node",
+  "missionClass": "fixer",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "etcd: etcd go client fails when querying a cluster with a down node",
+    "description": "etcd go client fails when querying a cluster with a down node. This issue affects 19+ users.",
+    "type": "troubleshoot",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Identify etcd troubleshoot symptoms",
+        "description": "Check for the issue in your etcd deployment:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n etcd -l app.kubernetes.io/name=etcd\nkubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=etcd -n etcd --tail=100 | grep -i error\n```\nLook for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review etcd configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant etcd configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get all -n etcd -l app.kubernetes.io/name=etcd\nkubectl get configmap -n etcd -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=etcd\n```\nThe etcd go client fails if multiple https endpoints are specified when the client is initialised and the first etcd endpoint is unavailable.\n\n**To Reproduce**\nI setup an etcd cluster with http (port 2378) and https (port 2379) listeners."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for etcd go client fails when querying a cluster with a down…",
+        "description": "Experiencing the same issue - however with SRV discovery which will use FQDNs for the endpoint list not IP addresses.\n\nSimilarly only the first endpoint will ever successfully be balanced.\n\nMy client is Hashicorp vault also, so related to https://github.com/hashicorp/vault/issues/4961.\nUsing `env \n```yaml\nctx, _ := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), requestTimeout)\n\n\tcfg := clientv3.Config{\n\t\tEndpoints:   []string{\"https://10.53.82.119:2379\" ,\"https://10.53.82.150:2379\", \"https://10.53.82.157:2379\"},\n\t\tDialTimeout: 5 * time.Second,\n\t}\n\n\tcert := \"/home/liam/tls_vault_certs/etcd-cert.pem\"\n\tkey := \"/home/liam/tls_vault_certs/etcd.key\"\n\tca := \"/home/liam/tls_vault_certs/etcd-ca.pem\"\n\ttls := transport.TLSInfo{\n\t\tTrustedCAFile: ca,\n\t\tCertFile:      cert,\n\t\tKeyFile:       key,\n\t}\n\n\ttlscfg, err := tls.ClientConfig()\n\tcfg.TLS = tlscfg\n\n\tcli, err := clientv3.New(cfg)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tlog.Fatal(e\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Confirm etcd go client fails when querying a cluster with… is resolved",
+        "description": "Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:\n```bash\nkubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=etcd -n etcd --tail=50 --since=5m\nkubectl get events -n etcd --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10\n```\nConfirm that the issue symptoms are gone."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "Experiencing the same issue - however with SRV discovery which will use FQDNs for the endpoint list not IP addresses.\n\nSimilarly only the first endpoint will ever successfully be balanced.\n\nMy client is Hashicorp vault also, so related to https://github.com/hashicorp/vault/issues/4961.\nUsing `env  ETCD_CLIENT_DEBUG=on  GRPC_GO_LOG_VERBOSITY_LEVEL=INFO` has helped debug.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "ctx, _ := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), requestTimeout)\n\n\tcfg := clientv3.Config{\n\t\tEndpoints:   []string{\"https://10.53.82.119:2379\" ,\"https://10.53.82.150:2379\", \"https://10.53.82.157:2379\"},\n\t\tDialTimeout: 5 * time.Second,\n\t}\n\n\tcert := \"/home/liam/tls_vault_certs/etcd-cert.pem\"\n\tkey := \"/home/liam/tls_vault_certs/etcd.key\"\n\tca := \"/home/liam/tls_vault_certs/etcd-ca.pem\"\n\ttls := transport.TLSInfo{\n\t\tTrustedCAFile: ca,\n\t\tCertFile:      cert,\n\t\tKeyFile:       key,\n\t}\n\n\ttlscfg, err := tls.ClientConfig()\n\tcfg.TLS = tlscfg\n\n\tcli, err := clientv3.New(cfg)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tlog.Fatal(err)\n\t}\n\tdefer cli.Close()\n\tkv := clientv3.NewKV(cli)",
+        "**http client**:",
+        "2) client.dial in turn calls DialContext from  **./vendor/google.golang.org/grpc/clientconn.go**"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {
+    "tags": [
+      "etcd",
+      "graduated",
+      "orchestration",
+      "troubleshoot"
+    ],
+    "cncfProjects": [
+      "etcd"
+    ],
+    "targetResourceKinds": [],
+    "difficulty": "intermediate",
+    "issueTypes": [
+      "troubleshoot"
+    ],
+    "maturity": "graduated",
+    "sourceUrls": {
+      "issue": "https://github.com/etcd-io/etcd/issues/9949",
+      "repo": "https://github.com/etcd-io/etcd"
+    },
+    "reactions": 19,
+    "comments": 21,
+    "synthesizedBy": "copilot"
+  },
+  "prerequisites": {
+    "kubernetes": ">=1.24",
+    "tools": [
+      "kubectl"
+    ],
+    "description": "A running Kubernetes cluster with etcd installed or the issue environment reproducible."
+  },
+  "security": {
+    "scannedAt": "2026-04-03T06:32:15.784Z",
+    "scannerVersion": "cncf-gen-3.0.0",
+    "sanitized": true,
+    "findings": []
+  }
+}

I am debugging around https://github.com/etcd-io/etcd/issues/9949.\nAnd resolve https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/72102.\n```yaml\nctx, _ := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), requestTimeout)\n\n\tcfg := clientv3.Config{\n\t\tEndpoints:   []string{\"https://10.53.82.119:2379\" ,\"https://10.53.82.150:2379\", \"https://10.53.82.157:2379\"},\n\t\tDialTimeout: 5 * time.Second,\n\t}\n\n\tcert := \"/home/liam/tls_vault_certs/etcd-cert.pem\"\n\tkey := \"/home/liam/tls_vault_certs/etcd.key\"\n\tca := \"/home/liam/tls_vault_certs/etcd-ca.pem\"\n\ttls := transport.TLSInfo{\n\t\tTrustedCAFile: ca,\n\t\tCertFile:      cert,\n\t\tKeyFile:       key,\n\t}\n\n\ttlscfg, err := tls.ClientConfig()\n\tcfg.TLS = tlscfg\n\n\tcli, err := clientv3.New(cfg)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tlog.Fatal(e\n```"

+        "description": "Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:\n```bash\nkubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=etcd -n etcd --tail=50 --since=5m\nkubectl get events -n etcd --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10\n```\nConfirm that the issue symptoms are gone."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "Log only when it errors + some clarification.\n\nFYI. I am debugging around https://github.com/etcd-io/etcd/issues/9949.\nAnd resolve https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/72102.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "ctx, _ := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), requestTimeout)\n\n\tcfg := clientv3.Config{\n\t\tEndpoints:   []string{\"https://10.53.82.119:2379\" ,\"https://10.53.82.150:2379\", \"https://10.53.82.157:2379\"},\n\t\tDialTimeout: 5 * time.Second,\n\t}\n\n\tcert := \"/home/liam/tls_vault_certs/etcd-cert.pem\"\n\tkey := \"/home/liam/tls_vault_certs/etcd.key\"\n\tca := \"/home/liam/tls_vault_certs/etcd-ca.pem\"\n\ttls := transport.TLSInfo{\n\t\tTrustedCAFile: ca,\n\t\tCertFile:      cert,\n\t\tKeyFile:       key,\n\t}\n\n\ttlscfg, err := tls.ClientConfig()\n\tcfg.TLS = tlscfg\n\n\tcli, err := clientv3.New(cfg)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tlog.Fatal(err)\n\t}\n\tdefer cli.Close()\n\tkv := clientv3.NewKV(cli)",
+        "**http client**:",
+        "2) client.dial in turn calls DialContext from  **./vendor/google.golang.org/grpc/clientconn.go**"
+      ]
